moduleDescription:
The strtabs module implements an efficient hash table that is a mapping from strings to strings. Supports a case-sensitive, case-insensitive and style-insensitive mode.String tables can be created from a table constructor:When using the style insensitive mode (modeStyleInsensitive), all letters are compared case insensitively within the ASCII range and underscores are ignored.An efficient string substitution operator % for the string table is also provided.See also:
  • tables module for general hash tables
  • sharedtables module for shared hash table support
  • strutils module for common string functions
  • json module for table-like structure which allows heterogeneous members

entries.name: StringTableMode
entries.code:
StringTableMode = enum
  modeCaseSensitive,        ## the table is case sensitive
  modeCaseInsensitive,      ## the table is case insensitive
  modeStyleInsensitive       ## the table is style insensitive

entries.name: StringTableObj
entries.code: StringTableObj = object of RootObj

entries.name: StringTableRef
entries.code: StringTableRef = ref StringTableObj

entries.name: FormatFlag
entries.code:
FormatFlag = enum
  useEnvironment,           ## Use environment variable if the ``$key``
                             ## is not found in the table.
                             ## Does nothing when using `js` target.
  useEmpty,                 ## Use the empty string as a default, thus it
                             ## won't throw an exception if ``$key`` is not
                             ## in the table.
  useKey                     ## Do not replace ``$key`` if it is not found
                             ## in the table (or in the environment).
entries.description: Flags for the % operator.

entries.name: mode
entries.code:
proc mode(t: StringTableRef): StringTableMode {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: pairs
entries.code:
iterator pairs(t: StringTableRef): tuple[key, value: string] {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over every (key, value) pair in the table t.

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys(t: StringTableRef): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over every key in the table t.

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values(t: StringTableRef): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over every value in the table t.

entries.name: len
entries.code:
proc len(t: StringTableRef): int {.gcsafe, extern: "nst$1", raises: [],
                                   tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(t: StringTableRef; key: string): var string {.gcsafe,
    extern: "nstTake", raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Retrieves the location at t[key].

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised. One can check with hasKey proc whether the key exists.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code:
proc getOrDefault(t: StringTableRef; key: string; default: string = ""): string {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Retrieves the location at t[key].

If key is not in t, the default value is returned (if not specified, it is an empty string ("")).

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code:
proc hasKey(t: StringTableRef; key: string): bool {.gcsafe, extern: "nst$1",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc
  • contains proc

entries.name: contains
entries.code:
proc contains(t: StringTableRef; key: string): bool {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`(t: StringTableRef; key, val: string) {.gcsafe, extern: "nstPut",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: newStringTable
entries.code:
proc newStringTable(mode: StringTableMode): owned(StringTableRef) {.gcsafe,
    extern: "nst$1", noSideEffect, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Creates a new empty string table.

See also:

  • newStringTable(keyValuePairs) proc

entries.name: newStringTable
entries.description:
Creates a new string table with given key, value string pairs.

StringTableMode must be specified.

entries.name: newStringTable
entries.description:
Creates a new string table with given (key, value) tuple pairs.

The default mode is case sensitive.

entries.name: clear
entries.code:
proc clear(s: StringTableRef; mode: StringTableMode) {.gcsafe, extern: "nst$1",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Resets a string table to be empty again, perhaps altering the mode.

See also:

  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear(s: StringTableRef) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Resets a string table to be empty again without changing the mode.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del(t: StringTableRef; key: string) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Removes key from t.

See also:

  • clear proc for resetting a table to be empty
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table

entries.code:
proc `$`(t: StringTableRef): string {.gcsafe, extern: "nstDollar", raises: [],
                                      tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The $ operator for string tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.
The % operator for string tables.
